channel.title: Chicken on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Chicken on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Zesty Asian Chicken Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Isabelle Dorval

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup rice vinegar
  • 8 tsp reduced sodium soy sauce
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp cilantro, chopped
  • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1/2 tsp sesame oil
  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
  • 4-3 oz boned and skinned chicken breast halves
  • 2 cups broccoli florets
  • 2 carrots, cut into 1 1/2 inch long matchsticks
  • 1 cup (6 ozs.) pitted prunes, snipped into halves
  • 3 green onions, sliced
  • 4 lettuce leaves

Instructions

To make dressing/marinade, in medium bowl whisk vinegar, soy, cilantro, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and pepper flakes. Remove 1/4 cup of mixture into a shallow 8 inch baking dish. Place chicken breasts in baking dish. Turn to coat both sides. Let chicken marinate for 30 minutes.

item.title: Chicken Tetrazzini
item.description:
Submitted by Christine Shank

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 boiling fowl (5 ½ - 6 lbs)
  • a few stalks of celery, chopped
  • 3 small onions, chopped
  • 1 quart milk
  • ¼ lb butter
  • 4 ozs hard flour
  • 2 cups chicken stock
  • 1 lb spaghetti
  • 1 lb mushrooms
  • ½ cup parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Boil fowl. Take meat from bones and put stalks of celery, onions and salt and pepper to taste into chicken stock. Simmer.

item.title: Angel Hair Pasta with Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Allison and Dave Rudback

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp olive oil, divided
  • 2 boneless chicken breasts, cubed
  • 1 carrot, sliced diagonally
  • 1 cup broccoli florets
  • 2 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 12 oz angel hair pasta
  • 2/3 cup chicken broth
  • 1/4 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1 tsp dried basil

Instructions

Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a medium sized skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towel.

item.title: No Peek Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Lacey Pigeau

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cups white rice
  • 1-10 oz can condensed celery soup
  • 1-10 oz can condensed mushroom soup
  • 1 pkg dried onion soup
  • 1 cup milk
  • 4-5 skinless chicken breasts

Instructions

Mix soup, milk and rice. Place mixture in a casserole dish. Lay chicken on top. Cover with foil and cook at 325 degrees for 2 hours. Do not peek!

item.title: Manilla Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Stephanie Lemaire and John Breton

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup onion, finely chopped
  • 1/3 cup white vinegar
  • 1/3 cup soya sauce
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 2 tbsp granulated sugar
  • 1/8 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Arrange chicken breasts in a single layer in a large saucepan. Mix remaining 8 ingredients in a small bowl. Pour over chicken. Cover. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for

item.title: Chicken Parmesan
item.description:
Submitted by Charlene and Leonard Gillis

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • ¼ cup parmesan cheese
  • 2 cups tomato sauce (or leftover spaghetti sauce)
  • ½ - 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese

Instructions

Put ½ cup of sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place the chicken on top of the sauce and pour remaining sauce over chicken.

Sprinkle parmesan cheese over the sauce and chicken and place in a preheated 350 degree oven. Bake for about 1 ½ hours or until chicken is tender when poked with a fork.

item.title: Chicken à la King
item.description:
Submitted by Rachelle and Tom Pigeau — Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups chopped, cooked chicken breast
  • Frozen vegetables (optional)
  • 1 can cream of chicken soup
  • 1/2 can milk (or water)
  • 1/2 cup mozzarella cheese, shredded
  • 1 pouch Stove Top stuffing mix
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Layer chicken and frozen vegetables in a medium baking dish.

Mix cream of chicken soup with 1/2 can of milk (or water) and pour over top of chicken and vegetables.

item.title: Becky's Sweet and Sour Chicken Wings
item.description:
Submitted by Becky and Darren Shackleton

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 large package of wings (about 25)
  • ¾ cup of soya sauce
  • ½ cup ketchup
  • ½ cup water
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 2 tsp flour

Instructions

Take a large glass casserole dish and mix soya sauce, ketchup, water and brown sugar. Add the flour and mix well. Add the wings in the dish and place in oven for 40 minutes at 400 degrees.

item.title: Cache Bay Chicken and Spinach Roll-Ups
item.description:
Submitted by Lorraine and Frank Nault

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 1 cup spinach, chopped
  • 1/4 cup Dijon mustard
  • 1/2 cup yogurt
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp black pepper
  • 1/4 cup lemon juice
  • 4 large boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 6 slices Swiss Cheese
  • 4 thin slices smoked turkey breast
  • 4 slices lemon (optional)

Instructions

In a bowl combine garlic, spinach, mustard, yogurt, salt, half the pepper and 1 tsp of lemon juice. Gently pound the chicken breasts flat between sheets of plastic wrap. Spread the sauce on the chicken. Top each breast with a slice each of the Swiss cheese and smoked turkey. Cut remaining slices of cheese in half and set aside.

item.title: Corey's Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 1 cup water
  • ½ tsp garlic
  • 5 Tbsp soya sauce

Instructions

Wash and place wings in pan. Melt sugar in water, Add garlic and soya sauce. Marinade for 1 hour. Bake uncovered at 350 degrees for 2-1/2 to 3 hours till sauce is thick and bubbly.

Stir every ½ hour.

item.title: Chicken & Dumplings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 5-1/2 to 6 lb boiling fowl
  • 8 cups water
  • 5-6 medium-large carrots, chunked
  • 12 whole medium onions
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

In large soup kettle, simmer the whole fowl with the water, covered, until tender – about 4 hours. Be sure it is beginning to fall apart. Left bird to utility tray and cut it into serving pieces discarding what skin and bones you readily can. Set this meat aside for the time being. Skim as much surplus fat as you can from the top of broth.NOTE;I usually do the above a day early so refrigerated fat is easy to scoop off the next day. Add the carrots, onions, salt and pepper and cook until vegetables are tender. Put chicken pieces in stew and when stew is boiling hard, put in dumplings.

item.title: Chicken A La Chuk
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Chicken legs, breasts or whatever
  • 1 tin beef consommé or broth
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ½ tsp ginger
  • ½ tsp garlic salt
  • 2 Tbsp soya sauce

Instructions

Heat consommé, brown sugar, ginger, garlic salt and soya sauce on stove. Put chicken in broiler pan and pour sauce over. Bake at 350 F. for 45 to 60 minutes.

item.title: Campbell's Shortcut Chicken a la King
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • ½ cup chopped red or green pepper
  • ½ cup chopped celery
  • 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup
  • 1/3 cup milk2 cups cooked chicken, cubed
  • 1 cup frozen green peas
  • dash ground black pepper, optional
  • 4 cups hot cooked rice or pasta

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add pepper and celery and stir fry until tender crisp. Reduce heat to medium.

Stir in mixture of soup and milk Add chicken, peas and pepper. Heat through, stirring occasionally. Serve over rice or pasta. If desired, garnish with fresh parsley.

item.title: Campbell's Easy Santa Fe Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp all-purpose flour
  • 1 Tbsp chili powder
  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves (about 1 lb/500 gr.)
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 lg red or green pepper, cut in strips
  • 1 can Fiesta Tomato or Tomato Soup
  • 1 cup corn niblets
  • ¼ cup shredded Cheddar or Monterey
  • 4 cups hot cooked rice
  • Jack cheese

Instructions

Mix flour and chili powder. Coat chicken with flour mixture.

Heat oil in large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside, Drain fat.

item.title: Campbell's Easy Chicken Broccoli Paprikash
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves,(about 1 pound/500 grams)
  • 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup
  • 2 tsp paprika (or to taste)
  • ½ medium red pepper, cut in strips
  • 2 cups hot cooked broccoli florets
  • 1/3 cup sour cream or plain yogurt
  • 4 cups hot cooked egg noodles

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside. Reduce heat to medium.

item.title: Campbell's Easy Chicken & Broccoli Divan
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups hot cooked broccoli spears
  • 2 cups cooked chicken, cubed*
  • 1 can Cream of Broccoli Soup
  • 1/3 cup milk
  • ½ cup shredded Cheddar cheese
  • 3 Tbsp dry bread crumbs
  • 1 Tbsp margarine or butter, melted

Instructions

*For Easy Turkey & Broccoli Divan, substitute turkey for chicken.

Arrange broccoli and chicken in 9-inch pie plate or 2 quart shallow baking dish. Combine soup and milk; pour over broccoli and chicken.

item.title: Campbell's 2-Step Honey Mustard Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves
  • 1 Tbsp oil
  • 1 can Condensed Cream of Chicken Soup
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 3 Tbsp Dijon-style mustard
  • 1 Tbsp honey

Instructions

Brown 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves in 1 Tbsp hot oil in skillet.

Add 1 can Condensed Cream of Chicken Soup mixed with 1/2 cup water, 3 Tbsp Dijon-style mustard and 1 Tbsp honey. Cover and simmer until chicken is cooked through.

item.title: Campbell's 2-Step Lemon Broccoli Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves
  • 1 Tbsp oil
  • 1 can Condensed Cream of Broccoli Soup
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 1 Tbsp lemon juice
  • Lemon slices for garnish

Instructions

Brown 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves in 1 Tbsp hot oil in skillet.

Add 1 can Condensed Cream of Broccoli Soup mixed with 1/2 cup water and 1 Tbsp lemon juice. Cover and simmer until chicken is cooked through. Garnish with lemon slices.

item.title: Campbell's Easy 2-Step Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless chicken breasts
  • 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup
  • 1/2 cup water

Instructions

Brown 4 boneless chicken breasts.

Add 1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup and 1/2 cup water. Cover and simmer until done.

Serves 4.

item.title: Campbell's Easy 2-Step Creamy Chicken & Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb boneless chicken breasts, cut up
  • 1 pkg frozen vegetable pasta blend
  • 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup
  • 1/2 cup water

Instructions

Brown 1 lb cut-up boneless chicken breasts.

Add 1 pkg frozen vegetable pasta blend, 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup and 1/2 cup water. Simmer until done.

Serves 4.

item.title: Campbell's Citrus Skillet Chicken & Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves,(about 1 lb/500 gr.)
  • 1-1/4 cups Campbell’s Ready to UseBroth or 1 can Condensed ChickenBroth.
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • ¾ cup orange juice
  • 1 cup uncooked regular long grain rice
  • 1-1/2 cups fresh (cut-up) or frozen greenbeans
  • 1 tsp grated orange peel
  • 3 Tbsp chopped fresh parsley or l Tbsp dried parsley flakes

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside.

item.title: Campbell's Chicken & Vegetable Rice Skillet
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 lb Boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves, cut in cubes
  • 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 pkg (6 oz/165 g) seasoned long-grainrice mix
  • 2 cups thinly sliced carrots
  • 2 green onions, sliced

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high heat in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on all sides. Reduce heat to medium.

Stir in mixture of soup, water, rice mix and carrots. Heat to a boil. Reduce heat to low.

item.title: Campbell's Chicken & Vegetable Rotini
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts,cut in chunks
  • 1 can Condensed Cream of Celery Soup
  • 2-1/4 cups water2 cups fresh (cut-up) or frozen veggie mix
  • ½ tsp dried basil leaves
  • 2 cups uncooked rotini pasta

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and stir fry until cooked through.

Stir in mixture of soup, water, vegetables and basil; heat to a boil, stirring often.

item.title: Campbell's Broccoli Chicken Potato Parmesan
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 8 mini red-skinned potatoes, thinly sliced
  • 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves, cut in strips
  • 1 can Cream of Broccoli Soup
  • ½ cup milk
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • 2 cups fresh or frozen broccoli florets
  • 1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add potato slices and stir fry until tender crisp.

Add chicken and continue to stir fry until chicken is browned on all sides. Reduce heat to medium.

item.title: Zesty Barbecued Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup no-salt added tomato sauce
  • 2 tsp minced garlic
  • ¼ cup plus
  • 2 Tbsp lemon juice
  • 1-1/2 tsp prepared mustard
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp Brown Sugar Twin
  • ¾ tsp ground red pepper
  • 3 Tbsp cider vinegar
  • ¼ tsp black pepper
  • 2 Tbsp low-sodium Worcestershire sauce
  • 6 (6-oz) skinned chicken breast halves

Instructions

Combine first 9 ingredients in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes. Pour 1 cup tomato sauce mixture into a large heavy-duty, zip-top plastic bag. Set aside remaining tomato sauce mixture. Add chicken to bag; seal bag, and shake until chicken is well coated. Marinate in refrigerator 8 hours, turning occasionally. Remove chicken from marinade, discarding marinade. Coat grill rack with cooking spray; place on grill over medium-hot coals (350 to 400 degrees). Place chicken on rack, and grill, covered, 8 minutes on each side or until chicken is done, basting frequently with reserved tomato sauce mixture.

item.title: Dip'n Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 250 gr tub spreadable cream cheese
  • ½ to ¾ cup sour cream
  • 1 cup salsa
  • 1-1/2 cups grated Cheddar cheese
  • 1 cup cubed cooked chicken (use left-over chicken or grill up a chicken breast)
  • 1 bag bite-size Nacho chips

Instructions

Preheat oven of 350 degrees. Blend cream cheese with sour cream until mixture is spreadable. Spread mixture into a 6-cup oven-safe serving dish.

Spread salsa over mixture, using enough salsa to cover evenly. Arrange chicken, then the cheese evenly over salsa.

item.title: Crispy Oven-Fried Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup plus 2 Tbsp frozen egg substitute, thawed
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 Tbsp water
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1 cup crispy rice cereal, crushed
  • 1 (3-lb) broiler-fryer, cut up and skinned
  • 1/3 cup toasted wheat germ
  • ¼ all-purpose flour
  • 1 Tbsp instant minced onion Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ tsp salt-free herb and spice blend

Instructions

Combine egg substitute and water in a shallow dish. Combine cereal and next 6 ingredients in a shallow dish. Place chicken and flour in a large zip-top plastic bag; seal bag, and shake until chicken is well coated. Dip chicken in egg substitute mixture; dredge in cereal mixture. Place chicken on rach of a broiler pan coated with cooking spray. Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 1 hour or until chicken is tender.

item.title: Chicken Vegetable Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cup water
  • 1 cup sliced carrot
  • 1 Tbsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules
  • 1 (8-oz)can mushroom stems and pieces,
  • 1 (14-1/2 oz) can no-salt added whole drained tomatoes, undrained and chopped
  • 1 cup diced zucchini
  • ¼ cup dried minced onion
  • 1 cup cooked chicken breast (skinned before cooking and cooked without salt or fat)
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • ¾ tsp minced garlic
  • 2 Tbsp dry red wine

Instructions

Combine first 7 ingredients in a Dutch oven. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes. Add carrot; cover and simmer 10 minutes. Add mushrooms and remaining ingredients; simmer, uncovered, 8 minutes. Yield: 7 (1 cup) servings.

item.title: Streamlined Honey Garlic Drumsticks
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup honey
  • 3 Tbsp soy sauce
  • 2 cloves garlic
  • 1 tsp ground ginger or 1” fresh ginger root
  • 1 tsp (any kind) vinegar
  • ¼ cup water
  • 2 lb drumsticks (about 12)

Instructions

Put the honey, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, vinegar and water into the container or a blender and whirl it around till smooth. Arrange drumsticks in 9” x 13” pan and pour sauce on top.

Bake at 350 F. for about an hour, basting from time to time with the sauce and turning the legs over about halfway through.

item.title: Russian Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Prep: 10 min. Total Time: 55 min.

Ingredients

  • 2 tsp vegetable oil
  • 6 small boneless skinless chicken breast halves (about 1-1/2 lbs.)
  • ½ cup Kraft Russian Dressing
  • ½ cup Kraft Pure Apricot jam
  • ½ pkg dry onion soup mix (about 3 Tbsp.)

Instructions

PREHEAT oven to 350 F. Heat oil in large skillet on medium-high heat. Add Chicken; cook 4 min. on each side or until browned. Remove chicken from skillet; place in 3-L baking dish.

item.title: Oven Barbecued Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

Preparation: 5 minutes Cooking: 30 minutes

  • 1 can condensed tomato soup
  • 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ tsp hot pepper sauce (optional)
  • 2 Tbsp packed brown sugar
  • 4 chicken breast halves (about 2 lbs)

Instructions

  1. In a small bowl, combine soup, vinegar, oil, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder and hot sauce.

  2. Remove skin from chicken. On rack in broiler pan, arrange chicken bone side up. Brush chicken with sauce. Broil 6” from heat for 25 to 30 minutes or until no longer pink, turning once and brushing often with sauce during cooking. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice if desired.

item.title: One Pan Cacciatore Dinner
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb chicken pieces
  • Flour (for coating)
  • A few Tbsp olive oil
  • Handful of sliced peppers
  • Handful of sliced mushrooms
  • 1 can (19 oz) Aylmer Accents Italian Stewed Tomatoes
  • 2 cups cooked spaghetti noodles
  • Parmesan cheese for topping

Instructions

Coat chicken pieces in flour and brown in a few tablespoons of olive oil.

Add sliced peppers and mushrooms; cook until tender, stirring occasionally.

Pour in one can of Italian Stewed Tomatoes, cover and simmer for 30 minutes or until done.

item.title: Glazed Chicken With Julienne Veggies
item.description:
Ingredients

Preparation Time: 10 minutes - Cook Time: 25 minutes

  • 2 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tin condensed chicken broth
  • 4 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves
  • ¼ cup apple juice
  • 1 large carrot cut into thin strips
  • 1 Tbsp honey
  • 1 medium green pepper, cut in thin strips
  • 2 tsp Dijonaise mustard

Instructions

  1. In small bow, stir together cornstarch, broth, apple juice, honey and mustard – set aside.

item.title: Honey Hoisin Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 Tbsp hoisin sauce
  • 2 Tbsp liquid honey
  • 2 Tbsp Ketchup
  • 2 minced garlic cloves
  • ½ tsp sesame oil
  • 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts

Instructions

In small saucepan, combine soy sauce, hoisin, honey, ketchup and garlic. Cook over medium heat for 2 minutes, stirring often. Remove from heat: stir in sesame oil. Remove ¼ of the sauce for basting; set remaining sauce aside.

Place chicken on greased grill over medium heat; close lid and cook for 2 minutes. Turn and brush with sauce, cook for 3 to 4 minutes on each side or until no longer pink inside. Serve with reserved sauce. Makes 2 servings.

item.title: Chili Flappers
item.description:
Preparation: 10 minutes Baking time: 30 minutes

Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 lb chicken wings
  • ½ cup dry bread crumbs
  • ¼ cup flour
  • 1 Old El Paso Chili Seasoning Mix (35gr pkg)

Instructions

Heat oven to 350 degrees. Line a baking sheet with foil, grease lightly. With a sharp knife, remove wing tips, discard. Cut through wings at joint to yield two pieces each. Place bread crumbs, flour and chili seasoning mix in large plastic bag, shake to combine. Shake chicken wings in mix to coat thoroughly.

item.title: Chuk's Chicken Fried Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 2 green peppers
  • 2 onions
  • 2 cups celery
  • mushroom pieces and stems
  • 1/2 cup soya sauce
  • 2 tins consommé soup
  • 4 cups minute rice

Instructions

Saute diced green peppers, chopped onions, chopped celery, mushroom in butter til onions are

transparent. Add soya sauce and soup. Bring this mixture to a boil and stir in minute rice.

Cover and cook on low heat for 1/2 hour.

Cooked chicken chunks, or ham, or egg can be added when rice is added.

item.title: Oxo Honey Garlic Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs chicken wings
  • 1/3 cup honey
  • 2 tbsp Oxo chicken bouillon liquid
  • 2 tbsp soya sauce
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • ½ tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ tsp ground ginger

Instructions

Cut wings in two sections and remove tips. In a large baking pan, place wings in a single layer. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and raise temperature to 475 degrees. Drain fat from pan.

item.title: Crispy Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs chicken wings
  • ½ cup melted butter or margarine
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • 2 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 1 cup dry bread crumbs
  • ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Cut chicken wings in two pieces, discarding the tips. In a small shallow bowl, combine butter and garlic powder. In another bowl, combine remaining ingredients. Dip chicken into butter mixture, then into crumb mixture. Place on greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 50 – 60 minutes or until done.

item.title: Oven-Baked Chicken
item.description:
• 1/3 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 3/4 tsp seasoned salt
  • 3/4 tsp salt
  • 1/4 tsp pepper
  • 12 chicken drumsticks, skin removed

Combine first 5 ingredients in large resealable freezer bag. Dampen chicken with water. Add a few pieces to bag. Seal bag. Shake until chicken is well coated. Arrange chicken in single layer on greased baking sheet. Repeat with remaining chicken and spice mixture. Bake in 425 degree Fahrenheit oven for 30 minutes. Turn chicken. Bake for about 15 minutes until juices run clear. Serves 4.

item.title: Honey Garlic Chicken Wings
item.description:
• 10 to 12 chicken wings, cut into 2 pieces
  • 3/4 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp corn starch
  • 3/4 tbsp soya sauce
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 2 cloves garlic, chopped very fine (or use a garlic press)

Brown chicken wings or spareribs in frying pan. Drain fat and place in casserole dish. In a separate pot, combine sauce ingredients. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Pour sauce over wings in casserole dish. Cook in microwave for 10 to 12 minutes on power level 6 or bake in oven at 325 to 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 to 40 minutes.

item.title: Buffalo Chicken Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 chicken breast
  • 1 brick of cream cheese, softened
  • 1/2 cup of ranch dressing
  • 1/2 cup (or more) of Franks Red Hot
  • 1 cup cheddar cheese, shredded
  • Salt to taste

Instructions

  1. Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, and chili flakes.  Bake in the oven or a pan.
  2. Shred the chicken and add the Frank’s Red Hot to it.  Mix it up.  (This can be left in the fridge overnight or can go to the next step right away).
  3. In a separate bowl, combine the cream cheese and ranch dressing.  Mix until mostly smooth.
  4. Add in the chicken/hot sauce mixture with a 1/2 cup of the cheddar cheese and mix till combined.
  5. Put the dip in a baking pan (8x8 works well) and cover with the remaining cheese.
  6. Bake at 350 for about 20 mins or until it starts bubbling.

item.title: Beer-Can Chicken
item.description:
• 1 can (12 oz.) beer
  • 1 chicken (3 1/2 to 4 lbs)
  • 2 tbsp or your favourite chicken rub (or Basic Barbecue Rub)

Pop the tab off the beer can. Using a church key-style can opener, make a few more holes in the top of the can. Pour out half the beer into your mouth and enjoy!

Set up the grill for indirect grilling (ie: hot side not where you’re putting the bird) and preheat to medium. If using a charcoal grill, place a large drip pan in the centre.

item.title: BBQ Chicken
item.description:
• 2 lb chicken breasts
  • 2 tbsp shortening
  • 1 can tomato soup
  • 1/2 cup chopped onion
  • 1/2 cup chopped celery
  • 2 tbsp brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 2 tbsp lemon juice
  • 2 tsp dry mustard

Brown chicken in shortening. Stir in other ingredients. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes until chicken is tender. Serve on a bed of cooked rice.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • Considering all of the strong flavoured ingredients in this, it didn’t turn out as strong as I thought it would. Still was pretty good though.
  • 4 out of 5

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Melt In Your Mouth Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup Greek yogurt (or mayo if you’re going a little less healthy)
  • 1/2 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1/2 tsp seasoned salt
  • 1/2 tsp black pepper
  • 1 tsp garlic powder

Instructions

  1. Mix mayonnaise or yogurt, cheese and seasonings.
  2. Spread mixture over chicken breasts and place in baking dish.
  3. Bake at 375°F for 30 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food.com:

Melt-In-Your-Mouth Chicken Breasts Recipe - Food.comThe mayonnaise make these chicken breast very moist and tender–enjoy!

item.title: Teriyaki Chicken Sushi Roll
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Chicken breast
  • Sesame oil
  • Olive oil
  • Sea salt
  • Thick teriyaki sauce
  • Nori sheet
  • 120 g of cooked, seasonedsushi rice
  • Sliced cucumber batons
  • ½ peeled avocado

NOTE: There are a lot of recipes to make the actual sushi rice, but I’ve usedTHIS ONE(made in the Instant Pot). It turns out pretty good.

The instructions for this one are a bit detailed to paste here, so please follow the link below to the original recipe from MakeSushi.com:

item.title: Spicy Chicken Sushi Roll
item.description:
Ingredients

Spicy Chicken

  • 1 cup shredded chicken (canned or rotisserie)
  • 3 ½ tablespoons mayo, more to taste
  • 1 tablespoon Sriracha

Sushi

  • 2 sheets seaweed, cut in half
  • 1 ½ cups cooked rice (short grain preferred)
  • Rice seasoning
  • 1 Persian cucumber, sliced (or regular cucumbers)
  • 1 large avocado, sliced

NOTE: There are a lot of recipes to make the actual sushi rice, but I’ve usedTHIS ONE(made in the Instant Pot). It turns out pretty good.

item.title: Hurry Up Chicken Pot Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2cups chopped cooked chicken breasts
  • 2hard-boiled eggs, sliced
  • 1⁄2cup thinly slicedcarrot
  • 1⁄2cupfrozen green pea
  • 1(10 3/4 ounce) can cream of chicken soup
  • 1cupchicken broth
  • salt and pepper (optional)
  • 11⁄2cups instantbiscuit mix
  • 1cupmilk
  • 1⁄2cup meltedbutter

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
  2. In a greased 2-quart casserole, layer the chicken, eggs, carrots, and peas.
  3. Mix the soup, chicken broth, and season with salt and pepper, if desired; pour over the layers.
  4. Stir together the biscuit mix and milk, and pour this over the casserole.
  5. Drizzle butter over the topping.
  6. Bake until the topping is golden brown, 30 to 40 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Paula Deen:

item.title: Hearty Chicken and Vegetable Skillet
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 red bell pepper, chopped
  • 1 zucchini, chopped
  • 1 (14.5 ounce) can diced tomatoes, undrained
  • 1/2 cup chicken broth
  • 1 tablespoon tomato paste
  • 1 teaspoon dried Italian seasoning
  • 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley, for garnish

Instructions

  1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
  2. Cook the chicken:Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper. Add them to the hot skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
  3. Sauté the vegetables:Add the chopped onion and red bell pepper to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the chopped zucchini and cook for an additional 3-5 minutes, until slightly tender.
  4. Build the sauce:Stir in the diced tomatoes (with their juice), chicken broth, tomato paste, Italian seasoning, and smoked paprika. Bring the sauce to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
  5. Return the chicken:Add the cooked chicken pieces back into the skillet with the vegetable and tomato sauce. Stir to combine and heat through for a couple of minutes.
  6. Serve:Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.

item.title: Crispy Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Dozen chicken wings
  • 1 tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsps garlic powder
  • Pinch of pepper

Instructions

Adjust oven rack to upper-middle position and preheat oven to 450°F (230°C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminium foil and set a heat-proof wire rack inside.

Pat dry chicken wings liberally with paper towels, squeezing out as much moisture as you can. Transfer them to a large bowl.

In a small bowl, combine the baking powder, garlic powder, salt and pepper together, whisking well to combine, and sprinkle the mixture over the wings. Toss wings through the baking powder mixture until evenly coated.

item.title: Baked Cream Cheese Chicken Taquitos
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8-10 flour or corn tortillas*
  • 1 cup shredded chicken
  • 2 oz. cream cheese softened (reduced fat will work)*
  • 1/2 cup shredded Monterey-jack cheese
  • 1 clove garlic minced (1/2 tsp.)
  • 1/4 cup salsa verde (I used normal red salsa)
  • 1/4 tsp. ground cumin (I didn’t use this)
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or foil (spray well if using foil) and set aside.
  2. In a large bowl, mix together the chicken, cream cheese, Monterey-jack cheese, garlic, salsa verde, and ground cumin until well combined. Season with salt & pepper to taste.
  3. Spoon two tablespoons of the chicken mixture into each tortilla. Roll each tortilla tightly and place seam side down on the baking sheet. You can secure the tortillas with toothpicks to hold them together a little better.
  4. Bake for 15-17 minutes, broil for the last minute or two to get them a little more crispy.
  5. Serve immediately.

Recipe Notes

If using corn tortillas, make sure to microwave them for 45 seconds-1 minute. This will make them a little easier to work with and less likely to tear.

item.title: Chicken Bacon Ranch Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients:

  • 2 cups of shredded chicken (approximately 2 chicken breasts)
  • 2 tablespoons of dry ranch seasoning
  • 1 cup of pasta of choice (macaroni is good)
  • 3 1/2 cups of chicken or beef broth (beef gives more flavour)
  • 2 tablespoons of milk
  • 4 oz (half a brick) of cream cheese
  • 4-6 slices of bacon crumbled or sliced
  • 2 cups of shredded cheese of choice

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to450 degrees Fahrenheit and cook chicken breasts until fully cooked (about 15-20 minutes).
  2. Add shredded chicken and ranch seasoning into a bowl and combine.
  3. Add chicken, broth, and pasta into a large pot, stir, and bring to a boil.
  4. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and cover for 15-20 minutes until noodles are the preferred texture.

Note:Stir every so often to make sure noodles aren’t sticking to the bottom

item.title: Chicken Taquitos
item.description:
Ingredients

Chicken:

  • 3 large chicken breasts
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1/4 tsp chili powder
  • 1/4 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp cumin
  • 1/4 tsp salt

Filling:

  • 1 block cream cheese
  • 3 tbsp sour cream
  • 1/3 cup salso
  • 2 tsp cumin
  • 1 tsp chili powder
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 and a 1/2 cups of shredded cheese
  • 16-18 small flour tortillas

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to375 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix together chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, and salt. Drizzle chicken with olive oil and add seasoning mix to both sides of each chicken breast
  3. Cook the chicken for 20-25 minutes or until fully cooked (internal temp of 165 Fahrenheit). Shred once cooked using 2 forks.
  4. Turn the oven up to 425 degrees Fahrenheit
  5. Mix cream cheese, sour cream, salsa, cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, cheeses, and shredded chicken together in a bowl.
  6. Add a few tablespoons to each tortilla and roll them up.
  7. Place on a parchment lined baking tray or in the airfryer until golden brown (about 10-15 minutes).

item.title: Keto Pesto Chicken Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 25 oz. chicken thighs or chicken breasts
  • 2 oz. butter, for frying
  • 3 oz. red pesto or green pesto
  • 1½ cups heavy whipping cream
  • ½ cup pitted olives
  • 8 oz. feta cheese, diced
  • 1 garlic clove, finely chopped
  • salt and pepper

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
  2. Cut the chicken thighs or chicken breasts into bite-sized pieces. Season with salt and pepper.
  3. Add butter to a large skillet and fry the chicken pieces, in batches, on medium high heat until golden brown.
  4. Mix pesto and heavy cream in a bowl.
  5. Place the fried chicken pieces in a baking dish together with olives, feta cheese and garlic. Add the pesto.
  6. Bake in oven for 20-30 minutes, until the dish turns bubbly and light brown around the edges.

Billy’s Notes:This was actually really good. A bit runny, but when you’re “hangry”, it doesn’t matter. I used the green pesto and it made it look more like barf than food, so might be worth trying the red pesto instead.

item.title: Instant Pot Lemon Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6-8 boneless chicken thighs , skinless or with skin* (Billy’s Note:I used 3 chicken breasts)
  • sea salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoonred chili flakesoptional or to taste
  • 2 Tablespoons olive oil
  • 3 Tablespoons butter (swap with ghee for paleo OR Whole30)
  • 1/2 small onion chopped
  • 4 garlic cloves sliced or minced
  • Juice of 1 lemon
  • 2-4 teaspoonsItalian seasoning
  • zest of half a lemon
  • 1/3 cup homemade or low sodium chicken broth **PLEASE SEE NOTES BELOW
  • 2 Tablespoons heavy cream OR coconut cream
  • Chopped fresh parsley and lemon slices for garnish , if desired

Instructions

  1. Season the chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and chili flakes.

To cook in the Instant Pot:

item.title: Instant Pot Buffalo Chicken Meatballs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1.5 lb ground chicken
  • 3/4 cupalmond meal
  • 1 tsp sea salt
  • 2 garlic cloves minced
  • 2 green onions thinly sliced
  • 2 tbspghee
  • 6 tbsphot sauce
  • 4 tbspgheeor butter
  • Chopped green onions, for garnish

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, combine chicken, almond meal, salt, minced garlic cloves, and green onions.
  2. Use your hands to combine everything together, but be careful not to overwork the meat.
  3. Grease your hands with ghee or coconut oil, then shape the meat into balls 1-2 inches wide.
  4. Set yourInstant Potto sauté setting and add 2 tbsp of ghee.
  5. ***Working in batches, gently place the chicken meatballs in the Instant Pot to brown them. Turn them every minute until all sides are brown.
  6. While the meatballs are browning, combine hot sauce and 4 tbsp of butter or ghee and heat them in the microwave or the stove top until the butter is completely melted. Use a spoon to stir. This is your buffalo sauce.
  7. Place all the browned meatballs in the Instant Pot, then pour the buffalo sauce evenly over the meatballs.
  8. Screw on the lid to the Instant Pot, make sure that the pressure valve is set to “sealing,” then set it to “Poultry.”
  9. Once the meatballs are finished cooking (about 15-20 minutes), the Instant Pot will beep. If you are eating right away, hit “Cancel” then release the pressure valve, making sure your hand is away from the opening where the steam escapes. If not, the Instant Pot will automatically switch to the “Warm” setting for the next 10 hours and the pressure will slowly lower on its own.

Billy’s Notes: I made the mistake of using Frank’s Buffalo Chicken Sauce for this when I made it. The sauce fell apart. Even though it tasted ok, it was pretty gross. Make sure to use actual Frank’s Original hot sauce. I also just used butter for where it called for (not ghee). They were good though. Both a really tasty hot sauce recipe AND a good keto meatball recipe.

item.title: Buffalo Chicken Spaghetti Squash
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 whole spaghetti squash, about 3 pounds
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 2 cups cooked, chopped chicken
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar, divided
  • 1 cup shredded Monterey jack, divided
  • 2 ounces cream cheese, softened
  • ½ cup sour cream
  • 2 tablespoons ranch seasoning
  • ⅓ - ½ cup Frank’s buffalo sauce, to taste
  • Ranch and Buffalo sauce, for drizzling on top
  • Green onions, for sprinkling on top

To Cook The Spaghetti Squash:

Instructions

  1. To cook in the oven, preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Cut the squash in half lengthwise and scoop out the seeds with a spoon. Discard the seeds. Drizzle with olive oil and place face down on a baking sheet. Bake for 1 hour or until squash is fork tender.
  2. Alternately, makeInstant Pot Spaghetti Squash
  3. Once squash is cooked, shred the squash into long spaghetti-like strands by using a fork to scrape the squash widthwise.

For The Filling:

item.title: Baked Chicken Drumsticks
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 pounds chicken drumsticks
  • 2 tablespoons avocado oil
  • 1 teaspoon paprika
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon onion powder
  • 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
  • ½ teaspoon salt
  • ½ teaspoon cracked pepper

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees. Spray a baking sheet with non-stick spray.
  2. Add all of the ingredients to a large zip top bag and seal. Smush the chicken around in the bag to coat in the seasoning.
  3. Arrange chicken on the baking sheet and bake for 40-45 minutes or until a thermometer reads 165 degrees.

Credit for this recipe goes to That Low Carb Life:

item.title: Bacon-Wrapped Broccoli-Stuff Chicken
item.description:
Bacon-Wrapped Broccoli-Stuffed Chicken

Ingredients

  • 4 chicken breasts
  • 4 oz cream cheese
  • ¾ tsp minced garlic
  • ¼ cup of parmesan cheese
  • 1 ½ cups of chopped broccoli
  • 8 slices of bacon

Instructions

  1. Mix everything but the chicken and bacon together in a bowl
  2. Cut a slit in each chicken breast and stuff them with the good stuff
  3. Wrap each breast with 2 slices of bacon
  4. Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour

Credit for this great recipe goes to Sonya Howard, one of my camping pals!

item.title: Bacon, Cream Cheese, Cheddar Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 4 chicken breasts (use 4 thin chicken breasts, or use 2 large chicken breasts, sliced in half, horizontally)
  • salt and pepper
  • 6 oz cream cheese , cold, refrigerated, and sliced into 8 slices
  • 8 strips bacon , cooked, chopped
  • 1 cup Cheddar cheese , shredded

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 400 F.
  2. Grease the bottom of the casserole dish with olive oil. I used the the oval casserole dish measured 13 inches x 9 inches x 4 inches deep.
  3. Add chicken breasts to the casserole dish. Note about chicken: use 4 thin chicken breasts, or use 2 large thick chicken breasts, sliced in half horizontally each to make 4 thin chicken breasts.
  4. Sprinkle chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
  5. Top with cream cheese. Cream cheese should be cold, right out of the fridge, sliced into 8 thin slices.
  6. Top with chopped cooked bacon (drained from fat).
  7. Top with shredded Cheddar cheese.
  8. Bake, uncovered, for about 20-30 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through. The cooking time will depend on the thickness of your chicken breasts.

Credit for this recipe goes to Julia’s Album (juliasalbum.com):

item.title: Faux-tisserie Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 1/2 to 3 pound whole chicken
  • 2 Tablespoons olive oil {divided}
  • sea salt & black pepper, to taste
  • 1/2 medium onion, cut into quarters
  • 5 large cloves fresh garlic, peeled and left whole
  • southwest seasoning mix {recipe below} or 2 Tablespoons of your favorite seasoning {lemon-garlic, paleo powder, etc.}
  • 1 cup chicken stock/broth, or water

Southwest Seasoning

  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon onion powder
  • 1 teaspoon chili powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon cumin
  • 1/2 teaspoon basil

Instructions

item.title: Instant Pot Chicken and Dumplings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tsp Olive Oil
  • 1 Tbsp Butter
  • 1 small Onion, diced
  • 1 Bay Leaf
  • 1 cup Carrots, chopped (about 2 or 3 carrots)
  • 1 cup Celery, chopped (2 stalks)
  • 3 large cloves Garlic, pressed or minced (about 1 Tbsp)
  • 1 tsp Salt (more to taste)
  • 1/2 tsp Pepper
  • 1/4 tsp Poultry Seasoning
  • 1/2 tsp Sage, dried
  • 4 sprigs Fresh Thyme, 2 tsp leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried)
  • 1 2" sprig Fresh Rosemary, 1 tsp leaves, stripped off and minced (or 1 tsp dried & crushed)
  • 4 cups Chicken Broth, low sodium
  • 1 large Potato, chopped
  • 1 1/2 lbs Chicken Thighs, and/or Breasts, cut in slightly larger than bite sized pieces
  • 1 cup Frozen Peas (or mixed vegetables)
  • 1/2 cup Heavy Cream
  • Box of Bisquick

Instructions

  1. Turn the pressure cooker on to the Sauté setting. When the pot is hot, add the olive oil and the butter. Then add the onion and bay leaf. Stir.
  2. Add the carrot, celery, and garlic. Cook for a few minutes, scraping the bottom of the pot to get up any brown bits (deglaze).
  3. Add the salt, pepper, poultry seasoning, sage, thyme, and rosemary. Cook for a few seconds to let the spices become fragrant.
  4. Add the chicken broth. Put a glass lid on the pot and let the contents warm up to barely a simmer.
  5. Add the potato and chicken and stir.
  6. Add the dumplings.  Make them according to the Bisquick package and then drop heaping teaspoonfulls into the broth (not too large as they need to cook through, and they expand).
  7. Cancel the Sauté function.
  8. Place the lid on the pot and lock in place. Turn the steam release knob to the sealing position.
  9. Press the Pressure Cook/Manual button (or dial) and then the + or - button (or dial) to select 7 minutes.
  10. When the cooking cycle has finished, do a Controlled Quick Release of the steam/pressure. Start by using a wooden spoon or other long handled utensil to turn the steam release knob towards Venting, and start letting the steam out gradually.
  11. Do this several times, and back off if any of the sauce starts to spew out with the steam. Then open it all the way when you feel certain only steam will be coming out. It will take a couple of minutes to fully vent.
  12. When the pin in the lid drops down, open the lid carefully, and stir in the frozen peas or mixed vegetables. You may need to push the dumplings aside.
  13. Stir in the heavy cream and put the glass lid on to let it sit and heat through for 3-5 minutes.
  14. Taste and adjust salt, if needed.
  15. Serve.

Credit for this recipe goes to Simply Happy Foodie:

item.title: Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • 1 1/3 pound chicken wings about 12 wings
  • 4 tbs taco seasoning
  • 1 1/2 tsp smoked salt

Instructions

Instructions

  1. In a shallow dish mix the spices together
  2. Roll each wing in the spices gently, the spice don’t need to cover the whole wing
  3. In your instapot- add 1 cup of water
  4. Add a steamer basket to the bottom of your instapot
  5. Add your wings to the steamer basket. They can touch but should not be on top of each other. Instead I turned my on their ends to fit the 12
  6. Close and lock the lid. Make sure the pot is set to sealing. Hit Manual and set for 10 minutes at high pressure.
  7. When the pot has cooked for 10 minutes at high pressure, carefully with a pan holder or some hand protection vent the pot.
  8. Once the pot has come back to regular pressure open the lid and remove the wings to a baking sheet. Broil wings for 10 minutes for crispy skin.
  9. Enjoy!

Here’s a link to the actual website. Credit to A Real Food Journey:

item.title: Chicken Pad Thai
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tbspolive oil
  • 2chicken breasts, diced
  • 4 clovesgarlic minced(or 2-3 tsp of minced garlic)
  • 3 tbsplow-sodium soy sauce or tamari
  • 1/2 cupjarred pad thai sauce (I like Thai Kitchen brand)
  • 1 1/2 cupswater
  • 1/2 packagerice noodles
  • 1 cupCarrot matchsticks
  • 1/2 eachred and yellow pepper, sliced(We replaced these with a bunch of bean sprouts instead)
  • 4green onions, sliced
  • 1/3 cupchopped peanuts(We omitted these for obvious reasons - allergies aplenty!)
  • 1/3 cupfresh chopped cilantro

NOTE: We made a few changes to the original recipe.

item.title: Chicken (from frozen), Rice, and Carrots
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 and 1/4 cups long grain white rice
  • 2 cups unsalted chicken broth
  • 1 tsp. salt
  • 1/2 tsp. pepper
  • 4 (6-8 ounces each) individually-frozen, boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 2 tsp. Italian herb blend, or your favorite dried herb blend
  • 5 -6 large carrots, peeled and cut into 1” pieces

Instructions

  1. Measure rice into bottom of the Instant Pot.
  2. Add chicken broth and salt and pepper, stir to combine.
  3. Lay the frozen chicken breasts on top of the rice. Season the chicken breasts with the Italian herb blend or your favorite herb blend.
  4. Lay carrots on top of the rice.
  5. Cover pot, lock on the lid, and set the steam release valve toSealing
  6. SelectPressure Cookand set to 10 minutes.
  7. When the cooking cycle has finished, allow the pot to sit for 5 minutes, and then do a quick release by moving the valve toVenting.

NOTES

You can make this recipe with fresh chicken breasts as well. Set the cooking time to 7 minutes. Allow the pot to sit for 5 minutes, and then do a quick release by moving the valve toVenting.

item.title: Chicken Broccoli and Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp Butter
  • 1½ - 2 Pounds Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast cut into cubes
  • 2 Cloves Garlic
  • 1 Small Onion
  • 1 Tsp Salt
  • 3/4 Tsp Pepper
  • 1 Tsp Garlic Powder
  • 1 and 1/3 Cups Long Grain Rice
  • 1 and 1/3 Cups Chicken Broth
  • ½ Cup Milk
  • 1½ Tbsp Flour
  • 1-2 Cups Fresh Broccoli cooked
  • 1 and 1/2 - 2 Cups Mild Shredded Cheddar Cheese

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Turn Instant Pot onto saute. Add butter and heat until hot. When hot, add chicken, onion, and garlic.
  2. Cook chicken mixture until onion starts to get translucent. Add rice, broth and seasonings. Stir well.
  3. Whisk together milk and flour and set aside.
  4. Cook on manual high pressure for 5 minutes. When the timer is up perform a quick release.
  5. Immediately add milk and flour mixture and mix until it is well combined.
  6. Add broccoli and cheese and stir until well combined. Serve immediately.

Here is a link to the actual website.  Credit to I Don’t Have Time for That:

item.title: Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • 1 Tablespoon oil (I use avocado, but you can use coconut or canola - something with a high smoke point is best!)
  • 3 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (uncooked)
  • 1/4 teaspoon garlic salt per chicken breast (or you can use just garlic powder and regular salt)
  • dash black pepper
  • 1/8 teaspoon dried oregano
  • 1/8 teaspoon dried basil
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Preheat the sauté function on the Instant Pot at the highest setting, and add oil to the pot.
  2. Season one side of the chicken breasts.
  3. After the display reads “hot,” carefully add the chicken breasts, seasoned side down, to the pot. I use tongs to avoid hot oil splatter.
  4. Add seasoning on the second side.
  5. Cook about 3 to 4 minutes on each side, and remove from pot with the tongs.
  6. Add 1 cup water to the pot (may need more for 8 quart pots), plus the trivet.
  7. Place the chicken on the trivet.
  8. Lock the lid, and cook on manual high for 5 minutes.
  9. Allow the chicken to naturally release for 5 minutes, and then quick release the rest.
  10. Remove the chicken from the pot, and allow to rest for about 5 minutes before serving for maximum juiciness.

Here is a link to the actual website. Credit to A Pinch of Healthy:

item.title: Oregano, Sun-Dried Tomato, and Chicken Stuffed Baguette
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 (8 oz/250g) pkg cream cheese, softened
  • 2 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 2 tbsp dried oregano leaves
  • 1 tsp (or less) of black pepper
  • 2 cups cooked, diced chicken breast (about 2 5oz breasts)
  • 1 cup finely sliced strips of seeded zucchini (about 1 small)
  • 1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes, reconstituted, thinly sliced
  • 1/4 cup mayonnaise
  • 1 1/2 tsp grated lemon peel (1/2 a lemon)
  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1 Parisan bread (baguette?)

Instructions

  1. Mix cream cheese, garlic, oregano, and black pepper together using an electric mixer until well blended.  Set aside.
  2. In a separate bowl, stir remaining ingredients together, except the baguette, and set aside.
  3. Slice the baguette lengthwise from the side but not all the way through.  Carefully scoop out bread from inside using a spoon.
  4. Completely coat inside of baguette with cream cheese mixture ensuring the edges are well covered, reserving 2 tablespoons of the mixture.  Fill baguette evently with chicken mixture.
  5. Close baguette, using the reserved cream cheese mixture to coat the cut edges.  Wrap very tightly with plastic wrap to keep baguette edges closed.  Chill in the refrigerator for 12 hours or overnight.
  6. Before serving, unwrap baguette and cut 1" wide slices with a serrated knife.  Transfer slices to serving platter and add garnish.

item.title: Air Fryer Panko Chicken Thighs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs. Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs (approximately 4 thighs)
  • 2 large Eggs
  • 2/3 cup All Purpose Flour
  • 2/3 cup Cornstarch
  • 1 cup Panko Breadcrumbs
  • 1 teaspoon Paprika
  • 1 teaspoon Salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon Pepper
  • 1/2 tablespoon Garlic Powder
  • 1/2 tablespoon Onion Powder

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, mix flour, cornstarch, and spices together.
  2. In a separate small bowl, whisk eggs.
  3. In a third bowl large enough to dip a whole chicken thigh, pour in the Panko bread crumbs.
  4. Coat a piece of chicken in flour mixture, then dredge in the egg wash, and finish coating it with the panko crumbs. Place on a clean solid surface, such as a plate or cutting board.
  5. Repeat for each piece of chicken.
  6. Pre-heat the air fryer to 350 degrees F. (1-2 minutes depending on your air fryer model).
  7. Spray your air fryer basked and chicken thighs with olive oil spray. The main reason for nonstick cooking spray is it will help the chicken thighs crisp up more and keep them from sticking to the basket.
  8. Working in batches, place the thighs in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
  9. Air fry the chicken on 350F for 12 minutes. Use tongs to flip chicken and air fry for another 3-5 minutes to make sure all sides of the chicken are nice and crisp. Check the chicken with a meat thermometer to make sure the internal temperature reaches 165F.

Credit for this recipe goes to TopRockin.com:

item.title: Air Fryer Chicken Thighs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5-6 boneless skinless chicken thighs patted dry
  • 1 teaspoongarlic powder
  • 1 teaspoonsmoked paprika
  • ½ teaspoonoregano
  • ½ teaspoonsalt
  • ¼ teaspoonblack pepper

Instructions

  1. Pat chicken thighs dry with a paper towel, then transfer to a bowl.
  2. Combine all the seasonings together in a bowl, then pour them into the bowl with the chicken and use your hands to coat the chicken with the spices.
  3. Preheat your air fryer to 380° (mine took 3 minutes to preheat). Then, place chicken smooth side down into the air fryer, spray with nonstick spray/olive oil spray, and cook at 380° for 12 minutes. NOTE: every air fryer is different, so cook times may vary.
  4. Once the air fryer beeps, use kitchen tongs to flip the chicken over, and cook for an additional 6-8 minutes on the second side, or until chicken is cooked through.  (Billy’s note:  I only put them in for 4 additional mins instead of 6-8.  I think they’d have been too dry with that, so gauge them when you flip them.)

Credit for this recipe to goes to The Almond Eater:
